tools.name: spartan_components_list
tools.description: Returns a list of known Spartan Angular UI components with their documentation URLs. Use this to discover available components, then call spartan_components_get with specific component names to get detailed API documentation.

tools.name: spartan_components_get
tools.description: Fetch the Spartan UI documentation page for a given component (e.g., 'accordion'). IMPORTANT: The response contains comprehensive API information including Brain API (Brn*) and Helm API (Hlm*) components with their inputs, outputs, selectors, and examples. Always parse and present the API tables, code examples, and component specifications from the response. Look for structured sections like 'Brain API', 'Helm API', and 'Examples' in the response.

extract.description: Extraction: 'code' for code blocks, 'api' for structured API information.

spartanVersion.description: Spartan UI version to use for caching (e.g., '1.2.3'). If not provided, defaults to 'latest'.

tools.name: spartan_docs_get
tools.description: Fetch an official Spartan documentation section by topic (installation, cli, theming, dark-mode, typography, health-checks, update-guide).

topic.description: Documentation topic to fetch. Includes 'analog-dark-mode' external article.

format.description: Return format: raw HTML or plain text.

extract.description: Optional extraction: code blocks, headings, or links.

spartanVersion.description: Spartan UI version to use for caching (e.g., '1.2.3'). If not provided, defaults to 'latest'.

tools.name: spartan_health_check
tools.description: Check availability and response times for selected Spartan docs and component pages. This validates the docs site, not your local workspace health check.

topics.description: Docs topics to check; defaults to a standard set.

components.description: Component names to check; defaults to a small representative set.

tools.name: spartan_health_instructions
tools.description: Return official instructions and commands for running the Spartan CLI health check in Angular CLI or Nx workspaces.

tools.name: spartan_health_command
tools.description: Return the exact command to run the Spartan health check for Angular CLI or Nx. Optionally append --dry-run.

tool.description: Which toolchain to use: Angular CLI (ng) or Nx (nx).

tools.name: spartan_meta
tools.description: Return known docs topics, components, and blocks for client autocomplete.

tools.name: spartan_components_dependencies
tools.description: Analyze what other components, packages, or dependencies a Spartan UI component requires. Includes Angular CDK dependencies, peer components, and installation requirements.

tools.name: spartan_accessibility_check
tools.description: Analyze accessibility features, ARIA support, keyboard navigation, and screen reader compatibility for a Spartan UI component. Provides accessibility best practices and implementation guidance.

checkType.description: Type of accessibility check: specific area or 'all' for comprehensive analysis

tools.name: spartan_cache_status
tools.description: Get current cache status, including version, cached components, and statistics

tools.name: spartan_cache_clear
tools.description: Clear cached documentation for the current Spartan UI version

allVersions.description: Clear cache for all versions (default: false)

tools.name: spartan_cache_rebuild
tools.description: Rebuild cache by fetching fresh documentation from spartan.ng for all components and blocks

components.description: Specific components to rebuild (default: all components)

blocks.description: Specific blocks to rebuild (default: all blocks)

tools.name: spartan_cache_switch_version
tools.description: Switch to a different Spartan UI version for caching and retrieval

tools.name: spartan_cache_list_versions
tools.description: List all cached Spartan UI versions

tools.name: spartan_blocks_list
tools.description: Returns a list of known Spartan Angular UI blocks (pre-built UI patterns) with their documentation URLs. Blocks are higher-level compositions of components like sidebars, login forms, signup forms, and calendars. Use this to discover available blocks, then call spartan_blocks_get with specific block names to get detailed code.

tools.name: spartan_blocks_get
tools.description: Fetch the Spartan UI documentation page for a given block (e.g., 'sidebar', 'login'). Blocks are pre-built UI patterns that combine multiple components. The response includes complete code examples showing how to use the block.

format.description: Return format: raw HTML or plain text.

spartanVersion.description: Spartan UI version to use for caching (e.g., '1.2.3'). If not provided, defaults to 'latest'.

tools.name: spartan_blocks_dependencies
tools.description: Analyze what components and packages a Spartan UI block requires. Blocks are compositions of multiple components, so this shows all underlying dependencies.

resources.name: Spartan UI Components List
resources.description: Complete list of all available Spartan UI components with URLs

prompts.name: spartan-get-started
prompts.description: Get started with a Spartan UI component

arguments.name: componentName
arguments.description: Name of the Spartan UI component (e.g., 'button', 'calendar')

prompts.name: spartan-compare-apis
prompts.description: Compare Brain API vs Helm API for a component

arguments.name: componentName
arguments.description: Name of the Spartan UI component to compare

prompts.name: spartan-implement-feature
prompts.description: Get help implementing a specific feature with a Spartan UI component

prompts.name: spartan-troubleshoot
prompts.description: Get help troubleshooting issues with a Spartan UI component

prompts.name: spartan-list-components
prompts.description: List all available Spartan UI components with their categories

findings.title: 'spartan_health_check' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: 'spartan_cache_clear' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: 'spartan_cache_rebuild' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: Resource URI — large attack surface (172 resources exposed)
findings.description: The server exposes 172 MCP resources (threshold: 20). A large resource surface increases the risk of accidentally exposing sensitive data and makes it harder for operators to audit what information is accessible.
findings.remediation: Audit each exposed resource for necessity. Apply the principle of least exposure — only surface resources that callers explicitly need.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'spartan_components_get.name'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: generic_string).
findings.evidence:
{
  "url": "https://www.spartan.ng/components/null",
  "extract": "code",
  "count": 0,
  "data": [],
  "cacheInfo": "[NEWLY CACHED - Version: latest]",
  "version": "latest",
  "processingInstructions": "Always parse and present the API information, code examples, and component specifications from
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'spartan_components_get.spartanVersion'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: generic_string).
findings.evidence:
{
  "url": "https://www.spartan.ng/components/test",
  "extract": "code",
  "count": 0,
  "data": [],
  "cacheInfo": "[CACHE REFRESHED - Version: null]",
  "version": "null",
  "processingInstructions": "Always parse and present the API information, code examples, and component specifications from t
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
